popularStory.headline: Adelaide of Holland, Countess of Hainaut (Dutch: Aleide (Aleidis) van Holland; c.?1230-buried 9 April 1284)
popularStory.description: Adelaide of Holland, Countess of Hainaut (Dutch: Aleide (Aleidis) van Holland; c.?1230-buried 9 April 1284) was a Dutch regent. She was a daughter of Floris IV, Count of Holland and Matilda of Brabant …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen於1225年9月22日出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特時，她的父親Floris IV van Holland15歲，母親Mechteld van Brabant26歲。她於1246年10月25日與Jean Ier d'Avesnes在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇福藍福特 阿姆 麥結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1284年4月9日在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年58歲，埋葬在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen于1225年9月22日出生在荷兰努-布拉班特时，她的父亲Floris IV van Holland15岁，母亲Mechteld van Brabant26岁。她于1246年10月25日与Jean Ier d'Avesnes在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇福藍福特 阿姆 麥结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1284年4月9日在法国豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年58岁，埋葬在法国豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Aleid
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Form of   Adelheid  , influenced by the North German form   Aleit .

en.lifeSummary: When Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen was born on 22 September 1225, in North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Floris IV van Holland, was 15 and her mother, Mechteld van Brabant, was 26. She married Jean Ier d'Avesnes on 25 October 1246, in Frankfurt, Holy Roman Empire. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 9 April 1284, in Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 58, and was buried in Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen est née le 22 septembre 1225, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris IV van Holland, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Mechteld van Brabant, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Jean Ier d'Avesnes le 25 octobre 1246, à Frankfurt am Main, Saint-Empire romain. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 avril 1284, à Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France.

de.Frankfurt, Holy Roman Empire: Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich
de.lifeSummary: Als Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen am 22. September 1225 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Floris IV van Holland 15 und ihre Mutter Mechteld van Brabant 26. Sie hat am 25. Oktober 1246 in Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich Jean Ier d'Avesnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. April 1284 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen è nata il il 22 settembre 1225 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Floris IV van Holland, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Mechteld van Brabant, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Jean Ier d'Avesnes il 25 ottobre 1246, nel Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 9 aprile 1284, nel Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolta a Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwenが1225年9月22日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで生まれた時，父Floris IV van Hollandは15歳，母Mechteld van Brabantは26歳であった。1246年10月25日にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチフランクファート アム メインでJean Ier d'Avesnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1284年4月9日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードベレンシエンスで58歳で死去し，フランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードベレンシエンスにて埋葬された。

ko.Frankfurt, Holy Roman Empire: 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 프랭k퍼트 아 맹
ko.lifeSummary: Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen(이)가 1225년 9월 22일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Floris IV van Holland(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mechteld van Brabant(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1246년 10월 25일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 프랭k퍼트 아 맹에서 Jean Ier d'Avesnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 4월 9일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 발시네스에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 발시네스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen nasceu em 22 de setembro de 1225, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris IV van Holland, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Mechteld van Brabant, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean Ier d'Avesnes em 25 de outubro de 1246, em Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de abril de 1284, em Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen родилась 22 сентября 1225 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Floris IV van Holland было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Mechteld van Brabant, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean Ier d'Avesnes 25 октября 1246 г. в местности Франкфурт-на-Майне, хилигесь рoмищесь раич. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 9 апреля 1284 г. в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.22 SEP 1225–9 APR 1284: 22 de septiembre de 1225–9 de abril de 1284
es.Frankfurt, Holy Roman Empire: Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen nació el 22 de septiembre de 1225, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, su papá, Floris IV van Holland, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Mechteld van Brabant, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Jean Ier d'Avesnes el 25 de octubre de 1246, en Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 9 de abril de 1284, en Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en Valenciennes, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia.
